methodsAttempted.query: site:zu.ac.ae Zayed University (ChatGPT OR "generative AI" OR "artificial intelligence" OR AI) (policy OR guidelines OR academic integrity OR assessment OR students OR faculty)
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned mostly ZU Scholars research records plus one official Zayed University June 2025 news page about AI literacy, faculty training, and Digital Education Council initiatives.

methodsAttempted.query: site:zu.ac.ae/main/en Zayed University AI policy students academic integrity generative AI
methodsAttempted.notes: Official-domain search found newsletters, conference/research PDFs, a catalog, and faculty profile content, but no dedicated university AI policy page.
The public news page was fetched successfully over HTTP and is not disallowed by the retrieved robots.txt rules.

The ZU Scholars article page was checked as an official repository record and rejected as primary evidence because it is a research publication record, not institutional policy or student/faculty guidance.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found one official Zayed University news/advisory page supporting narrow claims about AI literacy programs and faculty training. Searches did not identify a dedicated university-wide AI policy page; research and conference records were treated as limited or rejected for policy evidence.

artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Zayed University news page dated 30 June 2025 describing DEC membership, an AI for Higher Education certificate, an AI Literacy for Students program, and a university-wide faculty training initiative. It is used only for narrow training/source-status claims, not as a binding AI policy.

artifacts.title: Zayed University staged AI policy source review
artifacts.summary: Official evidence supports narrow claims that Zayed University announced AI literacy and faculty training initiatives in June 2025. No dedicated official AI policy page was identified in this worker pass.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The verified source is an official news/advisory page; it does not establish binding AI-use permissions, prohibitions, or assessment rules.
